title: Discarded silkworm pupae can be used as high-nutritional-value livestock feed: CFTRI chief
content: ‘Dried silkworm pupae contain 55-60% protein, 30-35% lipids and smaller amounts of carbohydrates, vitamins and minerals’

title: Bengaluru man killed by nephew
content: The deceased, Inayath Pasha, was living alone on the ground floor of a building in Austin Town which belongs to his sister.

title: Bihar CM Nitish Kumar may step down, likely to go to Rajya Sabha; son Nishant Kumar likely to become Deputy CM
content: Meeting of top JD-U leaders, including the party’s working president Sanjay Kumar Jha and Union Minister and party’s MP from Munger Rajeev Ranjan Singh alias Lalan Singh are going on at the official residence of Chief Minister Nitish Kumar

title: ‘Operate buses to other states, districts from satellite stands’
content: Former corporator of MCC K.V. Mallesh feels this would considerably reduce the pressure on the suburban bus stand, city bus stand and the core areas of the city

title: UVI levels remain high in parts of Kerala, alerts issued in districts
content: According to a bulletin issued by theKSDMA on February 4, 2026, the highest level of UVI (eight) was recorded at Munnar in Idukki

title: APCC president Sharmila criticises Anganwadi workers’ arrest
content: The Chief Minister should honour the assurances given to Anganwadi workers when he was in the Opposition, she says, and vows to extend full support to their movement for protection of their rights

title: IISc and Pratiksha Trust launch moonshot project on brain co-processors
content: The primary goal of the moonshot project is to develop both implantable and non-invasive brain co-processors that can decode brain activity from neural recordings, process it with an AI algorithm, and re-encode signals back into the brain via neural stimulation or neurofeedback

title: Israel-Iran conflict: Oman Air restores flights halted for Ramzan offering relief to expats stuck in Gulf
content: Hundreds of Indian expatriates from the UAE are travelling to Muscat by road. The 450-km journey from Dubai takes about six hours, including roughly one hour for border clearance

title: Fifty years on, Rajan’s death continues to be shrouded in mystery
content: It was on March 1, 1976, that P. Rajan, a student of Regional Engineering College, Kozhikode, was taken into police custody alleging links with Naxalites. Despite a prolonged legal battle by his father Eachara Varier, Rajan’s whereabouts still remain a mystery. The police personnel accused of being involved in the incident have all along protested their innocence. The incident left a deep impact on the State’s politics too.

title: Govt. will showcase ‘Andhra model of education’ to the world, asserts HRD Minister Lokesh
content: It is striving to create 20 lakh jobs over the next five years and improve the skills of youth by soon launching the ‘Naipunyam’ portal and utilising AI for assessing performance of students, he tells the Legislative Assembly

title: Guest lecturers demand job security
content: Some of them have now been kept out of service because of UGC guidelines pertaining to their qualifying examination for lectureship

title: Inquiry needed against phone tapping, as it involves privacy issue, says Joshi
content: The Union Minister points out the contradiction in the statement of Home Minister G. Parameshwara on telephone tapping in the State

title: Assembly approves Fire Service (Amendment) Bill, 2026
content: It enables the government to issue Fire Safety Certificates in place of NOC / licences and consider all Fire Services as Emergency Services

title: Centre allocates ₹736 crore for drinking water, sewage projects in Anantapur
content: The funds have been allocated under AMRUT 2.0 and AIIB schemes by the Union government, says Anantapur MP

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: Kodikunnil Suresh invites G. Sudhakaran into UDF fold, slams CPI(M) for insulting senior leader
content: Expressing strong disapproval of the CPI(M) leadership’s current stance, the MP describes the neglect and derogatory remarks directed at Sudhakaran over the past few years as extremely unfortunate

title: Bengaluru techie ends life; dowry harassment angle being probed
content: The deceased, identified as Sushma, 35, was working with a private IT company and had been married to Puneeth Kumar for five years. The couple has a four-year-old son.

title: The human cost of sanitation
content: In this series, we trace the unseen lives of the State’s sanitation workers, capturing the human cost, systemic failures, and hidden labour that keeps the sanitation network functioning.

title: Eerie silence prevails in Nanguneri after double murder with caste overtone
content: Posters of ‘Manalmedu’ Shankar pasted around Nanguneri reportedly angered members of an intermediate caste and they attacked some people who were standing in front of a roadside teashop

title: Telangana man injured in attack near Abu Dhabi airport; parents in Sircilla seek safe return
content: Passengers who arrived at Hyderabad airport from Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, said they did not face any difficulties due to the prevailing situation in the Gulf region

title: Researchers in Kerala try out land-based nurseries for coral conservation
content: Coral species collected from underwater reefs off Vizhinjam, Thiruvananthapuram, are grown in a controlled environment on land as part of a Kufos project. They will be reintroduced into their natural habitat once they reach a certain level of growth

title: How the Telangana Women’s Rugby League is empowering girls through rugby
content: Young women from rural and small-town backgrounds are discovering confidence and opportunity through rugby training and league matches

title: Telangana CM’s remarks indicate lack of coordination among officials
content: CM Revanth Reddy more than once directed senior officials, district collectors in particular, to visit villages to understand ground level realities

title: CM Stalin releases Tamil Nadu Shipbuilding Policy, T.N. Agroforestry Policy 2026
content: Mr. Stalin laid the foundation stone for the first phase of works to develop the Guindy Eco Park. The park will be developed with features including theme gardens, a children’s play area, bird habitat islands, herbal gardens, an aviary habitat centre, lawns, viewing decks and gazebos.

title: Don't be swayed by 'misinformation campaign' by opposition on fuel shortage, price hike: BJP
content: “India has built a "resilient, diversified and forward-looking" energy security framework over the past decade”

title: Release date of Kannada film ‘Love Mocktail 3’ advanced after ‘Toxic’ delay
content: Directed by Darling Krishna and co-written by his partner Milana Nagaraj, ‘Love Mocktail 3’ will be distributed by KVN Productions, the banner behind ‘Toxic’ and ‘Jana Nayagan’

title: Kerala Assembly polls: RSP-Congress friction deepens in Eravipuram as Congress pushes Karthik Premachandran as candidate
content: Local Congress leaders demand that son of Kollam MP N.K. Premachandran be fielded to ensure UDF victory. Citing current nominee’s “low chances”, around 38 leaders petition Leader of Opposition V.D. Satheesan

title: Governors should examine Bills within reasonable time: Goa Governor
content: Goa Governor Pusapati Ashok Gajapathi Raju was speaking to a group of journalists from Kerala who visited the Lok Bhavan in Panjim last week as part of a press tour

title: Eminent journalist and former ambassador H.K. Dua passes away at 88
content: H.K. Dua breathed his last peacefully this afternoon at a private hospital, a member of his family said. Dua was keeping unwell and was admitted to the hospital three weeks back.

title: TVK chief Vijay sees DMK-AIADMK conspiracy to thwart his rise to power
content: Addressing supporters in Thanjavur, Vijay promised that educational expenses of the wards of farmers owning up to two acres of land and of landless labourers would be borne by the government if TVK is voted to power in the upcoming Assembly election

title: Bar on non-tribal candidates for Meghalaya tribal council polls stokes controversy
content: While Garo organisations have defended a February 17 notification making the ST certificate mandatory for all candidates, non-tribal leaders say the move is unconstitutional

title: Summer heat pushes up Kerala’s daily power consumption
content: As per Power department projections, average daily consumption in March is likely to touch 103.5 million units (mu), and that of April and May, 111.7 mu and 96.4 mu respectively

title: Watch: Explained: What are the new air ticket refund norms?
content: The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has amended the air ticket refund rules making it passenger friendly. Passengers can now cancel or amend ticket without any additional fee within 48 hours of booking.

title: How students’ resource maps can be a gamechanger in local body planning
content: Students of Government Model Boys’ Vocational and Higher Secondary School, Attingal, apply experiential knowledge to practical situations to come out with 26 thematic maps that can emerge as a valuable resource in Attingal municipality’s planning process

title: Israel-Iran War: Eight more stranded persons seek help to return to Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka
content: In all, a total of 17 persons from Dakhina Kannada have sought help of the Karnataka government to return from UAE, Qatar, and Saudi Arabia

title: Kerala CPI(M) veteran G. Sudhakaran signals break with party after 63 years
content: In a Facebook post, Sudhakaran says he has not applied for membership scrutiny (an annual intra-party exercise) because he feels the CPI(M)’s State and district (Alappuzha) leadership has ‘spurned and ridiculed him’

title: T.N. Chief Minister Stalin inaugurates auditorium at Presidency College
content: It has seating capacity for 1,272 persons on the ground floor and 800 persons on the first floor, along with an administrative office and a VIP room

title: India ranks second globally for children who are overweight and living with obesity: report
content: India stood second only to China in the number of children with high BMI: 41 million high BMI; 14 million obesity, as per the World Obesity Atlas 2026

title: Infiltration foiled along LoC in J&K’s Rajouri, says Army; search operation on
content: Suspected persons were observed trying to sneak into this side from the Turkandi forward area around 4.15 a.m., prompting the army to open fire, the officials said

title: Solve confusion in delivering postal items addressed to the dead: Madras High Court directs India Post
content: Chief Justice Manindra Mohan Shrivastava’s bench also orders that the postal items must be handed over to legal heirs present in the residence of the deceased until the DGPS takes a call

title: Farmer in Kerala’s Wayanad dies in suspected elephant attack
content: According to local residents, the victim had gone to drive off an elephant that had entered his farm. However, as he did not return, they checked and found him lying on the ground with elephant footprints around him

title: T.N. CM Stalin slams BJP govt. at Centre for use of Hindi names in Tamil, English scripts
content: Taking strong objection to the practice of writing Hindi names even in Tamil and English instead of using appropriate terms in the respective languages, Mr. Stalin, in a post on X, described the practice as “one language – three scripts” and said it was a form of Hindi imposition.

title: Congress dodges a bullet after dilly-dallying on alliance with DMK in Tamil Nadu
content: Alliance arithmetic has strained in recent days, especially with a section of the national party airing a preference for actor Vijay’s TVK

title: Rajya Sabha tangle: Negotiation continues in MVA, BJP issues list of four candidates
content: State Congress leadership wants the Rajya Sabha seat allocated to itself, but several leaders The Hindu spoke with, said they might have to support Sharad Pawar if he decides to contest

title: Govt closely monitoring West Asia turmoil; safety mechanisms activated for seafarers: Sonowal
content: Sonowal said his Ministry is prepared to extend all necessary operational, diplomatic and humanitarian support to safeguard Indian vessels and personnel

title: The grammar of belonging in Barpeta’s Dol Jatra
content: Barpeta’s Dol Jatra reveals how theology, ecology and shared labour can reimagine community in an age of fragmentation.

title: West Asia crisis: MEA establishes control room to assist Indians
content: The Control Room can be contacted from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. at: 1800118797 (Toll Free) +91 11 2301 2113, +91 11 2301 4104, +91 11 2301 7905

title: South Western Railway registers 14.31% growth, records overall gross revenue of ₹8,550 crore
content: Freight revenue reached ₹4,876 crore during the year, reflecting a significant growth of 18.27%, while passenger revenue increased to ₹3,154 crore, registering a growth of 9.25%

title: Pahalgam terror attack: NIA seeks China’s legal assistance to trace information on GoPro linked to attack
content: According to the application, the NIA, during the course of the investigation into the case, seized various material objects and electronic devices connected with the conspiracy and execution of the terrorist attack

title: Sabarimala gold case: Kerala court grants statutory bail to former TDB president A. Padmakumar
content: Having already secured bail in kattilappali (doorframe) case, the latest bail clears way for Padmakumar’s release from prison

title: Sharing video or photo of minor girl in Mallikarjun Mutya POCSO case will attract legal action by Yadgir police in Karnataka
content: Superintendent of Police Prutwik Shankar stated that sharing and uploading information or photos or videos of the minor girl, and identifying her parents by posting their photos, is illegal

title: EmpowHer 1.0 to help over 6,000 women entrepreneurs in Tamil Nadu
content: The inaugural cohort comprises 160 aspiring women entrepreneurs from Chennai, Kancheepuram, Chengalpattu, and Tiruvallur

title: Kerala private sector nurses go on strike; services at 490 hospitals and clinics hit
content: They seek higher pay, improved staffing levels to reduce workload, better patient-caregiver ratio and end to alleged arbitrary lay-offs

title: Centre sanctions ₹3,942 crore to boost digital connectivity in Chhattisgarh: CM Vishnu Deo Sai
content: As part of the ambitious project, 11,682 gram panchayats in the State will be connected through a “ring topology-based” network

title: KLETU team bags 2nd rank at national-level m-BAJA SAE India 2026 in Hyderabad
content: KLE Technological University’s ‘Team Euros Racing’ has secured All India Rank 2 at the m-BAJA SAE India 2026 competition held at BVRIT, Narsapur (Hyderabad, Telangana) from February 25 to March 1

title: TVK supporters target media outlets online, draw ire
content: They created several fake social media accounts in the names of media outlets, as well as many fictitious media organisations, and indulged in spreading false and defamatory remarks targeting journalists, Chennai Press Club said in a statement

title: Protests against Khamenei's killing: Curbs remain in force in Kashmir
content: As a precautionary measure, the government shut educational institutions till March 7, while mobile internet speed continued to remain throttled

title: Five from Bengaluru killed in a road accident in Chittoor
content: The car in which they were travelling rammed the rear of a stationary lorry on the highway. The impact of the collision was severe, resulting in the death of all five occupants

title: Trail of deadly blasts in Maharashtra’s Nagpur; former Home Minister demands safety audits
content: At least 10 factories in Nagpur’s Bazargaon area — including Solar Explosives Industries Ltd., Chamundi Explosives, and Asian Fire Works — that are explosives factories have been plagued by frequent blasts, the Anil Deshmukh said.

title: ‘Toxic’ postponed: Yash-starrer delayed due to “Middle East tensions”
content: ‘Toxic’, starring Yash and directed by Geetu Mohandas, was scheduled to hit the screens on March 19, 2026. The film is jointly produced by KVN Productions and Monster Mind Creations

title: Ex-Navy official arrested with 1.2 kg of ganja, cannabis plants in Kerala
content: The accused, identified as Binu V. William, was nabbed during a search conducted at his residence in Poomkavu based on a tip-off

title: Srinagar MP, ex-Mayor booked for circulating false content on social media
content: The FIR against Aga Ruhullah and Junaid Azim Matoo is egregiously unwarranted and unjust; must be withdrawn immediately, said Mehbooba Mufti

title: Minister tells officials to fill Hanchinal lake in Vijayapura with treated water
content: The Minister told officials to ensure that all feeding canals were cleared of encroachments to remove impediments to water flow to the lake

title: Delhi Police on high alert for Holi; 134 intersections under strict watch
content: A senior police officer said teams have been deployed at 134 major intersections to nab drunken driving culprits. Serious violation will invite suspension of driving licence for a minimum of three months

title: DMK to hold seat-sharing talks with Kamal Haasan‘s MNM on March 4
content: As the DMK had previously alloted a Rajya Sabha seat to Mr. Haasan, his party may be given only one Assembly seat

title: Jairam Ramesh flays BJP MPs' praise of Modi, compares Parliament to North Korea's Assembly
content: The Congress leader called it “unfortunate” that the Modi government is “totally allied with Israel” and “afraid of Trump”

title: Silent surge in districts: Door-to-door screening reveals uneven burden of NCDs in Karnataka
content: A senior official said the district-level variations underline the need for targeted public health strategies rather than a uniform approach

title: Men's Bracketology: Miami (Ohio) or Auburn? Sorry, Bruce Pearl -- it's the RedHawks
content: The RedHawks are two wins away from an undefeated regular season while the Tigers are barely above .500.

title: Wynn leaves Lady Vols; senior day 'breaking point'
content: Kaiya Wynn, a reserve guard on the Tennessee women's basketball team, said she is leaving the program, calling what she felt was a slight on senior day over the weekend a "breaking point."

title: Prosecutors to offer plea agreements in poker case
content: Prosecutors said on Tuesday that they expect to extend formal plea agreements to 12 of the 31 defendants in the federal case into rigged poker games involving Chauncey Billups and Damon Jones.

title: Hurley's ASU upsets Kansas in possible send-off
content: Arizona State coach Bobby Hurley tried to keep the focus on his players after an upset win over No. 14 Kansas in his possible final home game with the Sun Devils.

title: Redick calls sideline tiff with Luka 'very normal'
content: Lakers coach JJ Redick called his testy bench exchange with Luka Doncic over the weekend much ado about nothing, saying he didn't understand why it went viral.

title: Colts place rarely used transition tag on QB Jones
content: The Colts place the rarely used transition tag on QB Daniel Jones, preserving their right to match any offer sheet in free agency.

title: Donald to return as Europe's Ryder Cup captain
content: Luke Donald has decided to return as Ryder Cup captain for Europe with a shot at making history as the only captain to win three straight times.

title: Sources: Cardinals to cut QB Murray next week
content: The Cardinals informed quarterback Kyler Murray that they intend to release him on the first day of the league year next Wednesday, barring a trade between now and then, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Monday.

title: Plum, Stewart question PA's handling of CBA talks
content: WNBPA executive committee members Kelsey Plum and Breanna Stewart wrote a three-page letter Monday to players' union executive director Terri Jackson expressing "serious concerns about how the PA is handling the current negotiations" for a new CBA.

title: 🏀 Why Billups could be facing decades in prison
content: After a Hall of Fame NBA career, here's what we know about Billups' connections to Mafia-affiliated poker games and illegal gambling.

content: Here's what you need to know about 2026 free agency. We picked out everything from players who might break the bank to sleepers to watch.

content: Sunday's reveal put a new team in the top 16. The start of Champ Week will bring more answers as the bracket comes into focus.

content: From the locks to the long shots to those in between, we count Cooperstown candidates in this year's World Baseball Classic.

content: Grand prix starts have always held the potential for chaos, but this year there's almost guaranteed drama when the lights go out in Australia on Sunday. ESPN explains why.

title: Has Aston Martin's F1 super team turned into a disaster team?
content: Fernando Alonso described Martin as the "team of the future" -- but it heads to Australia in disarray.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Man United, Chelsea, Liverpool eye USMNT star
content: Manchester United, Chelsea, and Liverpool are monitoring the situation of Bournemouth and USMNT midfielder Tyler Adams. Transfer Talk has the latest.

content: Emanuel Navarette dominated Eduardo Nunez to keep his top spot at 130 pounds. Here are the latest ESPN divisional rankings.

content: Tristan H.Cockcroft highlights the players -- and stats -- that have grabbed his attention heading into the fantasy baseball season.

title: Qatar to fully shut gas liquefaction amid Tehran's retaliatory strikes
content: Amidst escalating Middle East tensions, Qatar is fully halting its crucial gas liquefaction operations on Wednesday. This significant move, driven by growing security concerns over regional conflict, will impact global energy markets.  Restarting these facilities is expected to take at least two weeks to reach full capacity, according to sources.

title: 'Scary time': How stranded passengers in Dubai used cabs, cash and detours to flee
content: Middle East air travel is severely disrupted following joint US-Israeli strikes on Iran and subsequent missile attacks. Thousands are stranded, resorting to overland journeys and expensive last-minute flights. Expatriates in Dubai face uncertainty, with some companies relocating staff. Despite escalating tensions, daily life in parts of the UAE continues with an air of normalcy.

title: Mark Zuckerberg is done with Meta's highest-paid employee, this reorganisation proves
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has quietly dismantled Alexandr Wang's power nine months after the $14 billion hire. The 28-year-old data labeling entrepreneur now oversees AI models being built on infrastructure he doesn't control. A new applied AI engineering organization under Andrew Bosworth routes talent and data pipelines around Wang entirely. Researchers report to other executives. Wang keeps his title but loses every lever that matters—a calculated pivot that suggests Zuckerberg's bet has already failed.

title: ATP cancels Fujairah matches; agree to fly players out after €5,000 charter backlash
content: ATP Tour has cancelled the Fujairah Challenger tournaments in the UAE after escalating Middle East tensions raised safety concerns. Players, officials and ball kids had earlier rushed for cover during a drone scare near the venue. The ATP also faced backlash for proposing a €5,000 charter flight for evacuation before reversing course and agreeing to cover players’ travel costs.

title: Middle East tensions bleed markets red: Sensex crashes 1,100 points; Nifty50 down 1%
content: Stock market crash today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex crashed in trade on Wednesday on rising Middle East tensions and escalating US-Israel-Iran war. While Nifty50 went below 24,350 in intraday trade, BSE Sensex went below 78,500.

title: OpenAI loses 1.5mn subscribers in less than 48 hours after Altman backs deal that Anthropic rejected
content: OpenAI faces a significant user exodus, with over 1.5 million reportedly leaving ChatGPT after agreeing to let the Pentagon use its AI on classified networks. This backlash, fueled by previous contracts and donations, has seen rival Anthropic's Claude chatbot surge in popularity. Users are now seeking ways to export their ChatGPT data and migrate to Claude.

title: 'A big concern': India bowling coach ahead of T20 WC semi-final
content: Ahead of India’s T20 World Cup semi-final against England at Wankhede, bowling coach Morne Morkel warned dew and small boundaries could be crucial factors. He urged bowlers to stay attacking despite tight margins. Head coach Gautam Gambhir said lower dew levels have tested India’s adaptability, stressing game awareness over strike rate in high-pressure World Cup matches.

title: After the Pentagon, OpenAI is reportedly looking at contract with Nato but there’s a Big difference
content: OpenAI is reportedly exploring a deal with NATO for its AI technology on unclassified networks, a distinction from its Pentagon contract for classified systems. This follows a Pentagon deal where OpenAI agreed not to use its AI for domestic surveillance of US persons. CEO Sam Altman acknowledged the negative PR but deemed the Pentagon decision "right."

title: Abhishek’s top spot under threat as Sahibzada Farhan’s twin tons shake ICC rankings
content: Pakistan's Sahibzada Farhan is now second in T20I batting rankings, just behind India's Abhishek Sharma, after scoring two centuries in the World Cup. Several Indian players, including Ishan Kishan and Tilak Varma, have entered the top 10. In bowling, Varun Chakravarthy leads, with Abrar Ahmed close behind. Sikandar Raza remains the top all-rounder.

title: MS Dhoni fined Rs 1000 after caught breaking traffic rules
content: MS Dhoni was fined Rs 1000 for overspeeding in Ranchi after an automated system issued an e-challan under the Motor Vehicles Act. He also recently received a notice over alleged misuse of a residential plot. Meanwhile, the 44-year-old is training with Chennai Super Kings, aiming for a stronger IPL 2026 after a poor 2025 season.

title: Inside Trump’s second-term military playbook: Strikes, raids and global interventions
content: The most dramatic escalation came on February 28, when the United States and Israel launched coordinated strikes across Iran targeting military installations and senior leadership. One strike hit a compound in Tehran housing the offices of the ayatollah, the Iranian presidency and the national security apparatus. The attack killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, sending shockwaves across the region.

title: When Rashmika Mandanna shared her emotional perspective on starting a family
content: In a throwback interview, Rashmika Mandanna made heartfelt reflections on motherhood and her now-husband Vijay Deverakonda. Following their dreamy Udaipur wedding on February 26, 2026, this interview has resurfaced where the 'Pushpa' star discussed her protective instincts for her future children and her plans for a balanced life in her 30s. On the work front, Rashmika Mandanna will be seen in 'Cocktail 2' with Shahid Kapoor and Kriti Sanon. She will also be seen in the period drama, 'Ranabaali' opposite Vijay Deverakonda.

title: 'Pakistan should've done something': Karthik slams England duo
content: After Pakistan’s Super 8 exit at the T20 World Cup 2026, Dinesh Karthik mocked Michael Atherton and Nasser Hussain for previously claiming India had an unfair venue advantage in the Champions Trophy. He said Pakistan enjoyed similar conditions but still failed, adding that by their “logic,” Pakistan should have done better, hinting at a “genuine lack of skill.”

title: Day 5: US assets hit across Mid-East; Israel launches fresh strikes on Iran, Lebanon
content: Intense fighting grips the Middle East as Iran and the US-Israeli alliance escalate conflict. Tehran faces sustained strikes on military and nuclear sites, retaliating with attacks on regional US assets and Israel. Casualties mount on both sides, with evacuations underway for foreign nationals. The future leadership of Iran remains uncertain amidst the escalating violence.

title: 'Target for elimination': Israel's warning for Iran's next supreme leader
content: Israel has warned that any successor to Iran's Supreme Leader will be a target for elimination, vowing to crush the regime's capabilities. This declaration follows the reported killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and the potential selection of his son, Mojtaba Khamenei, as his successor. The IDF is preparing for further actions amid ongoing airstrikes against Hezbollah and Iranian security sites.

title: 6 Vastu tips to keep in mind while designing a 3-BHK apartment
content: Designing a 3 BHK home goes beyond aesthetics, impacting family well-being and decision-making. Vastu principles, when applied, foster inner stability and future growth. Key areas like the entrance, primary bedroom, kitchen, children's rooms, living spaces, and clutter-free zones are crucial for promoting clarity, health, ambition, connection, and forward momentum, transforming a house into a launchpad for purposeful living.

title: Iran war worry for Himachal hoteliers: Will Israelis come this summer?
content: Hoteliers in Kullu Valley are anxiously monitoring the ongoing conflict, fearing a significant drop in Israeli tourist arrivals this summer.  These visitors are crucial to the region's hospitality industry, and a prolonged war could lead to substantial economic losses.  Currently, very few Israelis are present in the Kullu and Kangra districts.

title: Why the 'win the semifinal, win the trophy' trend matters in India vs England clash
content: India and England, both two-time T20 World Cup champions, clash in a highly anticipated semifinal at Wankhede Stadium. With a balanced semifinal record and a chance to become the first three-time winners, this encounter promises intense rivalry. Both teams have relied on collective efforts, setting the stage for a thrilling contest.

title: From protests to polls: Nepal to vote after a Gen Z uprising redraws its political map
content: Nepal is at a critical juncture as nearly 19 million voters elect a new House of Representatives. This election is significantly influenced by a youth-led uprising that challenged the political establishment, fueled by resentment over unemployment and perceived elite excess. The vote reflects a generational demand for accountability and change.

title: Ex-Pak captain who beat India in 2017 CT final in line to become head coach
content: Former Pakistan captain Sarfaraz Ahmed is reportedly set to be appointed as the head coach of the Test side. This move comes after the role remained vacant for nearly five months, with Azhar Mahmood temporarily overseeing the team. Sarfaraz's coaching credentials were bolstered by his success with the U-19 team, and an official confirmation is expected soon.

title: 'Pak will turn into vassal state': Asif accuses Israel of 'orchestrated war' that could align India
content: Pakistan's Defence Minister Khawaja Asif alleged Zionists are orchestrating the Iran war to expand Israel's influence towards Pakistan's borders. He claimed Zionism has controlled global economics for a century, influencing every catastrophe in the Islamic world. Asif also stated Afghanistan, Iran, and India share an agenda of enmity towards Pakistan, urging citizens to recognize this conspiracy

title: West Asia crisis: 38 Indian ships stuck in Persian Gulf; 3 sailors dead
content: Thirty-eight Indian-flagged ships, carrying crude and LNG with nearly 1,100 seafarers, are stranded in the Persian Gulf due to the West Asia conflict. Three Indian sailors died and one was injured on foreign-flagged vessels in recent attacks off Oman. Shipping authorities are prioritizing seafarer safety and maritime asset security.

title: 'Safety risks': US orders non-emergency staff in Pakistan to leave Karachi, Lahore consulates
content: Amid escalating tensions following US-Israeli strikes on Iran and Tehran's retaliation, the United States has ordered non-emergency staff and family members to depart its consulates in Lahore and Karachi due to safety risks. Washington also authorized voluntary departures from diplomatic missions in Saudi Arabia, Cyprus, and Oman as a precautionary measure.

title: Team India seeks blessings at Siddhivinayak ahead of T20 WC semifinal — Watch
content: Ahead of their T20 World Cup semifinal, Indian cricketers sought blessings at Mumbai's Siddhivinayak Temple. Despite a challenging tournament run, the team showed resilience, boosted by Sanju Samson's stellar performance. Facing a strong England side, India's key players and spin attack will be crucial for success on a potentially difficult pitch.

title: US military base, hotel in Iraq targeted; drone downed near Baghdad airport
content: Erbil, Iraq, witnessed drone attacks on a US military base and a hotel Wednesday. Near Baghdad International Airport, another drone was intercepted, with no reported casualties or damage. These incidents follow a pattern of increased targeting of US military sites in the region amid heightened US-Iran tensions, with Iran-aligned militias vowing to continue their action

title: Oil shock: Why Indian equities may be among most impacted in Asia by Iran war
content: India’s equity market, valued at around $5 trillion, has trailed most major global peers since late 2024 due to slower profit growth and limited participation in artificial intelligence-linked stocks.

title: 500 million and counting! T20 World Cup 2026 shatters viewership records in India
content: The ICC T20 World Cup 2026 has shattered viewership records in India, attracting over 500 million fans, the highest ever for the tournament. This milestone, celebrated by ICC chairman Jay Shah, highlights the event's global and accessible vision. Digital engagement also soared, with JioHotstar peaking at 60.5 million concurrent viewers as the tournament heads into its knockout stages.

title: 'Ashes to ashes, dust to dust': Inside Masaan Holi at Manikarnika Ghat
content: Banaras' Masaan Holi offers a unique, contemplative celebration with sacred ashes from cremation pyres, unlike the riot of colours elsewhere. Rooted in Shaivite traditions, it symbolizes life's cyclical nature and confronts mortality. While social media has amplified its spectacle, the ritual's profound, existential encounter remains, urging immersion beyond filtered frames.

title: 5 of Bengaluru family die after car rams stationary truck in Chittoor
content: A tragic accident on the Palamaneru highway claimed the lives of five family members traveling from Bengaluru to Tirupati. Their car collided with a stationary truck, with police suspecting overspeeding and driver fatigue as contributing factors. Relatives have been notified and are en route to the scene.

title: Kashmir protests over Khamenei’s killing: Meta blocks newspapers, MP booked for ‘misleading content’
content: Following protests over Iran's supreme leader's death, Jammu and Kashmir schools closed until March 7. Meta blocked local newspaper accounts, drawing criticism for censorship. Authorities registered cases against political figures for allegedly spreading misleading content, while the LG urged calm amidst ongoing demonstrations.

title: Middle East crisis: For families, reunions add colour to festivities at Delhi airport
content: Families in Delhi are celebrating Holi and Eid with renewed joy after reuniting with relatives who returned from West Asia amid escalating tensions. Passengers arriving at IGI Airport expressed relief at being back in India, emphasizing the safety of their homeland after experiencing a tense but manageable situation abroad.

title: ‘Toxic’ new release date: Fans laud Yash’s decision to postpone film amid Middle East tensions
content: Yash's 'Toxic' release is postponed by four months to June 4, 2026, due to Middle East tensions impacting global reach. Makers aim for a wider audience connection with this delay. Fans laud the decision, seeing it as a 'perfect move' that also avoids a box office clash with Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2', allowing both films to thrive.

title: Petronet LNG shares tumble 12% after Qatar production halt reports
content: Petronet LNG shares plummeted nearly 12% after reports of Qatar declaring force majeure on natural gas supplies due to an Iranian drone strike. This production halt has led to significant supply cuts for Indian industries, impacting sectors reliant on gas imports. The disruption also affected other gas distributors and is driving up global energy prices.

title: US planning to arm Kurdish forces to trigger uprising in Iran: Report
content: The CIA is reportedly planning to arm Kurdish forces in Iran to incite a popular uprising, with the Trump administration in talks with opposition groups and Kurdish leaders. This move aims to draw Iranian security forces away from urban centers, potentially aiding civilian protests and straining Tehran's military resources.

title: From record highs to 13% fall: How Middle East crisis hit South Korean benchmark Kospi
content: South Korea's Kospi index experienced a dramatic 12% tumble, triggering a trading suspension after a significant sell-off led by market heavyweights. This sharp decline follows a previous session's fall amid escalating Middle East tensions, raising questions about whether it's a pause or a turning point for the AI-fueled rally.

title: India sets up control room, issues helpline number amid Middle East crisis
content: Amid escalating West Asia tensions, India's Ministry of External Affairs has established a special control room to assist its citizens residing in the region. The government is closely monitoring the evolving situation and remains in contact with regional governments and international partners to ensure the safety of nearly one crore Indians living there.

content: a good chance that momentum continues into spring and the 2026 season . the ACC finished 8-6 against the SEC in 2025 and finished with nine teams that had eight or more wins . a 4-0 postseason mark against the sEC is the last in the league .

title: DMK's Sharp Attack At Centre After Hindi Board At Tamil Nadu Railway Office
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin has accused the BJP-led Union Government of crossing limits in its attempts to impose Hindi, saying the naming of the Regional Railway office entrance in Hindi reflects a "disgusting form of Hindi imposition".

title: Presidential Palace, Iran Guards HQ Hit: Satellite Pics Show Tehran Damage
content: High-resolution satellite imagery accessed by NDTV shows the scale of destruction inflicted on Tehran after a wave of coordinated American and Israeli strikes. The images -- captured before and after the bombardment -- show damage across sites.

title: "Next Mission Is Waiting For You": Israel Commander To F-35 Pilot On Radio
content: These things usually appear in memoirs, long after wars and battles end. One such moment likely unfolded today, if the details shared by the Israeli Air Force (IAF) are true.

title: US Struck Iranian Warship Off Sri Lanka Coast: Report
content: One of the officials, who was speaking on condition of anonymity, said it was carried out by a US military submarine.

title: Nitish Kumar May Quit As Bihar Chief Minister, Son To Get Deputy Post: Sources
content: The move to shift Kumar has been anticipated since before the 2025 assembly elections. But action was not taken given Kumar's illustrious track record.

title: Iran Fires Missile At Turkey, Destroyed By NATO Air Defence Systems
content: A ballistic missile fired from Iran and heading into Turkish airspace was destroyed by NATO air and missile defence systems in the eastern Mediterranean Sea, the Turkish Defence Ministry said on Wednesday.

title: Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Death Count In Iran From US-Israeli Attacks Rises To 1,045
content: Israel-Iran War live updates: An Iranian ballistic missile hit the United States' military base at Al-Udeid on Tuesday, Qatar's defence ministry said, as Tehran struck targets across the Gulf in retaliation for US-Israeli attacks.

title: Pak Could Be Next Country To Enter Iran-Israel War. Here's Why
content: The work done by a small Iranian suicide drone in Saudi Arabia may draw Pakistan, a country thousands of kilometres away, to the war in the Middle East.

title: Iran's Cluster Bomb Missiles Raise Questions Over Russia And China's Role
content: Israeli experts have suggested that external assistance may have played a role, raising speculation about possible transfers of military know-how from Russia or China.

title: Iran Postpones Funeral For Khamenei In Anticipation Of Unprecedented Turnout
content: Iran announced that a state funeral for supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, which had been planned for Wednesday evening in Tehran, was postponed "in anticipation of unprecedented turnout," state television reported.

title: 'Found A Few Bodies Of Sailors': Lanka After Submarine Attack On Iran Ship
content: Sri Lanka's foreign ministry said that the military had rescued 32 people on board the sinking Iranian ship. Dr. Anil Jasinghe, a top health ministry official, says one of them is in critical condition, seven are receiving emergency treatment.

title: Rupee Falls To All-Time Low Of 92.16 Against US Dollar Amid Middle East War
content: The rupee slumped 67 paise to close at an all-time low of 92.16 against US dollar on Wednesday, weighed down by spiking crude oil prices in the wake of the Iran crisis.

title: Iran To Allow Only Chinese Vessels Through Strait Of Hormuz: Sources
content: Iran has said it will allow only Chinese vessels to pass through the Strait of Hormuz as an expression of gratitude for Beijing's stand towards Tehran since the war in the Middle East began, sources have said

title: Buses, Taxis, Trucks Go Off Roads As Maharashtra Transport Strike Begins Tomorrow
content: The agitation is not limited to heavy trucks; it encompasses a broad coalition of school buses, private tourist buses, tempos, taxis, and autorickshaws, signaling a total shutdown of the state's transport machinery.

title: Saudi Aramco's Largest Refinery Hit Again By Drone
content: Oil giant Saudi Aramco's Ras Tanura, home to its largest domestic refinery, was struck again by a drone today, a Saudi Defence Ministry spokesperson said.

title: Maharashtra Minister Vs Ex-Cop Over Alleged Plan To Jail Bal Thackeray
content: In his biography Troubleshooter, Raghuvanshi has alleged that this incident took place during his tenure in the Special Task Force.

title: Why India Has Never Bought American Fighter Jets
content: Look at the skies over Iran right now. Almost every fighter aircraft you're seeing in those skies, at some point in the last 20 years, has been offered to India.

title: Man Who Fatally Shot A Cop With His Service Revolver Executed In Florida
content: A man convicted of fatally shooting a police officer with his own service weapon during a traffic stop was executed.

title: MMA divisional rankings: Kavanagh, Martinez flip the script in two top-10s
content: lone'er Kavanagh beats two-time men's flyweight titleholder Brandon Moreno . the 27-year-old from Mexico earned a top-10 position by defeating marlon Vera . ailin Perez leapfrogged Macy Chiasson into the top 5 after beating her by decision .

title: How Kyler Murray's exit from Cards impacts Vikings, draft
content: quarterback will be released on the first day of the 2026 league year . sources tell ESPN that he prefers two unnamed destinations . he's already guaranteed $36.8 million for 2026 . the Cardinals avoided paying him another $19.5 million .

title: Live NHL trade tracker for 2025-26: All the deals made before March 6 deadline
content: the trade deadline for the 2025-26 NHL season is Friday . the deals continue to roll in as contenders look to bolster their roster . you'll find information on every trade made since the start of the regular season .

title: NCAA men's hockey conference tournament schedules, results
content: the six league champions will earn automatic berths into the 16-team NCAA field . the other 10 spots going to the top teams in the NPI rankings . regional play in the tournament begins March 26 and culminates with the Frozen Four in Las Vegas on April 9 and 11 .

title: Wetzel: No tax on tips? How about 'no tax on NIL'?
content: high-paid athletes will keep paying the state 4% on everything you earn over $10,000 in 2026 . the rate is slowly dropping until it hits 3% in 2030 . "Soak the rich," is an old populist battle cry, is apparently the new one .

title: Tom Seaver memorabilia auction nets $1.8M, led by $854K 1969 WS ring
content: the late new york Mets legend's 1969 world series ring sold via Heritage Auctions for $854,122 . the record paid for any seaver collectible is the third-highest sum for any sports championship ring . Seaver, a 12-time All-Star and three-time NL Cy Young winner, died in august 2020 .

title: Stacking college football's 25 best newcomer classes
content: the transfer portal has changed how programs build rosters . some schools go all-in every offseason to overhaul their rosters through the portal . others use the portal to add depth to a position or target major transfers who could take a team from great to top-ranked.
